title: Jordan Park — Preventive plateau (visit 4)
description: Fourth PCP headache visit: propranolol failed after initial response, venlafaxine titrating up, PedMIDAS plateaued (42 → 36 → 35 → 33), school absenteeism continuing, pediatric neurology referral placed today.

history.label: Visit 1 note — first headache visit
history.text: First PCP headache visit. Recurrent migraine-pattern headaches, ~12 headache days/month, PedMIDAS 42. Started propranolol 10 mg daily after clinician review. Family reported initial improvement at the 2-week nurse check. Family history: father with migraine.

history.label: Visit 2 note — headache follow-up
history.text: Second headache visit. Initial response to propranolol faded after about 1 month; frequency back to ~12 days/month. PedMIDAS 36. Propranolol increased to 20 mg daily.

history.label: Visit 3 note — headache follow-up
history.text: Third headache visit. Brief response to propranolol 20 mg, then effect faded again after about a month. Frequency unchanged at ~12 days/month. PedMIDAS 35. Propranolol tapered and stopped; venlafaxine started after clinician review.

history.label: School attendance letter + parent call
history.text: School attendance letter: 6 full days and 8 partial days missed in the last 3 months due to headache. Parents called the clinic expressing worry that Jordan is falling behind at school.

history.label: Pre-visit PedMIDAS questionnaire (parent)
history.text: PedMIDAS completed by parent before today's visit: 6 full school days missed, 8 partial school days, 8 days functioning at less than half in school, 4 days unable to do home activities, 3 days missed play or social activities, 4 days participated at less than half. Total 33. Family continues to keep the headache diary.

history.label: Vitals (today)
history.text: BP 112/70, HR 72, T 36.7C, Ht 160 cm, Wt 52 kg. Appears well.

History of cancer, immune suppression, or shunt

turns.speaker: clinician
turns.text: Jordan, how have the headaches been since we started the venlafaxine?

turns.speaker: patient
turns.text: Honestly, about the same. Still around three days a week — some weeks a little less, but they always come back.

evidence.quote: Honestly, about the same. Still around three days a week — some weeks a little less, but they always come back.

treatment_response.value: Initial response to propranolol lost after ~1 month; no clear change yet on venlafaxine

turns.speaker: parent
turns.text: What worries us most is school. He's missed six full days this quarter and left early another eight times. He's falling behind, and he's stopped playing basketball with his friends.

turns.speaker: clinician
turns.text: That's a big burden. The attendance letter from the school matches what you're describing.

evidence.quote: What worries us most is school. He's missed six full days this quarter and left early another eight times. He's falling behind, and he's stopped playing basketball with his friends.

school_impact.value: Severe — 6 full and 8 partial school days missed in last 3 months; absenteeism continuing, parents worried

turns.speaker: clinician
turns.text: Any headaches that wake him from sleep, vomiting in the morning, vision changes, or any weakness or numbness?

turns.speaker: patient
turns.text: No, nothing like that. No waking up with headaches, no throwing up in the morning, my eyes are fine, no weakness or anything.

turns.speaker: clinician
turns.text: How often are you using ibuprofen when a headache hits?

turns.speaker: patient
turns.text: Maybe once or twice a week, only for the really bad ones.

turns.speaker: clinician
turns.text: We'll step the venlafaxine up to the next dose as planned, and I'm sending a referral to pediatric neurology today — after two preventives without lasting improvement, it's time to bring in a specialist.

brief_lines.text: 4th PCP headache visit: propranolol 10 → 20 mg failed after initial response; venlafaxine started 05/22, titrating up.
PedMIDAS plateaued: 42 → 36 → 35 → 33 — slight early improvement only, not meaningfully better.
Headache frequency essentially unchanged at ~12 days/month across all four visits.
School heavily affected: 6 full + 8 partial days missed in last 3 months; parents called expressing worry.
Neurology referral appropriate today — two adequate preventive trials without sustained response (clinician decision).
Neurology wait currently ~10-12 weeks — expedite request pending.

PedMIDAS plateaued 42 → 36 → 35 → 33; headache frequency essentially unchanged at ~12 days/month. [evc-2, evc-6, evc-9]
School burden severe and continuing: 6 full + 8 partial days missed in last 3 months; parents worried. [evc-4, evc-5, evc-10]

referral_considerations: Neurology referral placed today — preventive plateau after two adequate trials (clinician confirmed)

care_plan.disclaimer: Draft for clinician review. Final clinical decisions remain with the clinician.

canned_qa.answer: The referral was placed today because Jordan has had two adequate preventive trials without sustained response: propranolol (10 then 20 mg) helped briefly but faded after about a month each time, and PedMIDAS has plateaued at 42 → 36 → 35 → 33 with frequency unchanged at ~12 days/month. This meets a common threshold for pediatric neurology co-management — the decision was confirmed by the clinician today.

Two preventives so far: propranolol 10 mg was started 03/06 with initial improvement, increased to 20 mg on 04/10, but the response faded after about a month each time and it was stopped 05/22. Venlafaxine was started 05/22 after clinician review and is being titrated up today.

School impact is severe and continuing: the attendance letter documents 6 full and 8 partial days missed in the last 3 months, the parent reports Jordan is falling behind and has stopped basketball, and the parents have called the clinic expressing worry. This is reflected in the PedMIDAS responses (pm1=6, pm2=8).

No red flags were identified today: Jordan denies waking headaches, morning vomiting, vision changes, and weakness or numbness. The pattern is static rather than progressively worsening — frequency has stayed at ~12 days/month across four visits. Several screen items remain unasked and are marked unknown. Clinical judgment remains with the clinician.

insights.note: Established chronic migraine picture, refractory to first-line preventives — plateau after two adequate trials. Neurology co-management appropriate; clinician review required.

criteria.text: Not better accounted for by another ICHD-3 diagnosis

dxs.rationale: Phenotype stable across four visits at ~12 days/month — near-chronic frequency with plateaued disability despite two preventive trials. Amber reflects management difficulty, not diagnostic doubt.
Ibuprofen ~6 days/month — below overuse threshold. Included to document that overuse was screened and excluded.

txs.detail: Second preventive after adequate propranolol failure — monitor mood and side effects during titration (clinician review required).
Two adequate preventive trials without sustained response — referral sent today; expedite requested (~10-12 week wait).

txs.rec: Consider CBT / behavioral therapy alongside preventive
txs.detail: Behavioral components support durable response and may help plateaued disability (clinician review).

txs.rec: 504 accommodation letter to reduce absenteeism impact
txs.detail: Hydration, nurse access, make-up work support while treatment is optimized — draft ready for clinician review.
